item.title: Two-year bond yield dips below BB policy rate
item.description: The yield on two-year treasury bonds fell below the central bank's policy rate on Tuesday, as banks parked excess liquidity in government securities amid subdued private sector credit demand. The cut off yield, generally known as interest rate, on the Bangladesh Government Treasury Bonds (BGTBs) c

item.title: Export of green veg to ME halted
item.description: CHATTOGRAM, Mar 03: The export of vegetables from Bangladesh, particularly Chattogram, to the countries of the Middle East has been stopped suddenly due to suspension of flights triggered by the escalating Iran-Israel-US conflict. Exporters and shipping agents warn that prolonged instability could

item.title: Why the kitchen shouldn’t be a ‘Women-Only’ zone this Ramadan
item.description: At the time of Maghrib’s call to prayer, many households move quickly toward the iftar table. In numerous homes, however, one familiar scene unfolds, while the family gathers, one person remains in the kitchen, finishing dishes, pouring drinks, or clearing space on the counter. By the time sh

item.title: How Ramadan can enhance our productivity
item.description: The holy month of Ramadan is the ninth month of the Islamic lunar calendar, observed by Muslims worldwide as a time of fasting, prayer, reflection, and building community relations. It is the best of all months and the month of peace and devotion. People commemorate the month in which the Quran was

item.title: Weak demand drags Paramount Textile profit by 19pc
item.description: Woven fabric manufacturer Paramount Textile reported more than 19 per cent lower profit of Tk 207.78 million in its second quarter (October-December) of FY26 as its revenue declined amid weaker global demand. According to a disclosure on Tuesday, earnings per share fell to Tk 1.16 in the second qu

item.title: Drug-resistant fungal 'superbug' spreading in Dhaka ICUs
item.description: Candida auris, a hard-to-treat drug-resistant fungus, is spreading within the intensive care units (ICUs) in Dhaka hospitals, according to a study conducted by the icddr,b. The study findings show that this so-called 'superbug' is not limited to newborns, but it is also affecting other critically-

item.title: Visually-impaired readers deprived, as most books lack Braille editions
item.description: The Ekushey Boimela (Book Fair) offers a vibrant celebration of literature, drawing readers of all ages and tastes with its vast collection of titles across a number of stalls. Yet amid the festivity and abundance, a sense of incompleteness lingers, particularly near the Bangla Academy's exit gate,

item.title: JU announces 19-day closure
item.description: Jahangirnagar University (JU) has declared a 19-day holiday on the occasion of the holy month of Ramadan, Shab-e-Qadr, Jummatul Bida, Eid-ul-Fitr, and Independence and National Day. The decision was announced in an official notice signed by Acting Registrar Dr A B M Azizur Rahman. According to the

item.title: Japan EPA opens doors, reforms now vital
item.description: Business leaders and policy experts on Tuesday urged the government to tackle long-standing structural bottlenecks to fully capitalise on the newly signed Economic Partnership Agreement (EPA) with Japan, warning that the deal alone will not automatically boost trade and investment. They said that

item.title: Trump threatens 'to cut off all trade with Spain' over military base ban
item.description: US President Donald Trump threatened to impose a ​full US trade embargo on Spain on Tuesday after the European and NATO ally refused to let the US military use its bases for missions linked to ‌strikes on Iran. "Spain has been terrible," Trump told reporters during a meeting with German Chanc

item.title: Iranian drone hits US consulate in Dubai
item.description: An Iranian drone struck a parking lot outside the US consulate in Dubai on Tuesday, causing a small fire but no injuries, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said. Rubio told reporters at the US Capitol that all consulate personnel in Dubai were safe and accounted for, reports Al Jazeera. He added

item.title: Hezbollah claims rocket attack on Israeli naval base in Haifa
item.description: Hezbollah said Tuesday evening that it has launched a rocket attack on an Israeli naval base in the northern Israeli city of Haifa, describing the move as a response to ongoing Israeli airstrikes on Lebanese territory. In a statement, the group said its fighters targeted the naval base with a salv
